title: Unicode Variation Selector-15 and some of my tears
excerpt: From my phone, I noticed that my website was displaying footnote backlink glyphs as emoji in my RSS reader. It had not always been this way. I opened the same page from a desktop browser and saw what I expected to see: a text-like pictograph, in my article body’s font face. No emoji to be found. I checked the HTML document source:
content_html:
From my phone, I noticed that my website was displaying footnote backlink glyphs as emoji in my RSS reader. It had not always been this way. I opened the same page from a desktop browser and saw what I expected to see: a text-like pictograph, in my article body’s font face. No emoji to be found. I checked the HTML document source:

<a href="#fnref:1" class="reversefootnote" role="doc-backlink">
  ↩
</a>

The RSS feed XML contained the same. So, that can be presented as either text or an emoji? &x8617; is an HTML representation of the Unicode codepoint U+21A9, Leftwards Arrow with Hook. This led me to many relevant threads, pastes, and blog posts1 in which others had observed similar issues with Leftwards Arrow with Hook and other, similar navigation-decoration characters. We were all reacting to U+21A9 as rendered without a Unicode standardized variation sequence specified. So it’s up to whatever program is rendering U+21A9 to decide what to do. Which version, the text or the emoji, should U+21A9 render.

I found the correct solution and some less correct solutions to this issue. Before I describe them, I want to explore a slightly larger problem introduced by variation selectors.

The downstream effects

For meta-ish content characters like backlink glyphs, the way forward is clear for people who are technical: fix and/or configure the thing that builds your web pages. Or complain in the direction of the people who can fix and/or configure the thing.

For many of the other characters, it’s more complicated. Many of these characters are used in body content to be expressive. Rendering the text presentation instead of the emoji presentation, at worst, could be a mistranslation. The technical standard (see the emoji-data links, and specifically emoji-data.txt) lists over 1,000 Unicode codepoints that have both text and emoji presentations.

There is no obvious, cut-and-dry user experience for writers here. Pictographs and emoji are regular text as far as most programs are concerned, and variation selectors remain invisible unless you go looking. Did the author mean to present an umbrella ⛱︎ or an umbrella ⛱️ ? We don’t know what their writing tool presented to them during editing. They don’t know that we don’t see what they think we see when we’re reading the thing. In a perfect world, all software knows about and inserts variation selectors for the user. In reality, we know that software is bad.

I found documentation that indicates that the Unicode Technical Committee is aware that variation selectors are complicated,2 and maybe weren’t the best strategy.3 But what’s done is done and can’t be undone when it comes to the Unicode Standard.

The most correct solution

You can specify, in your source document, which version you want rendered. You do this by combining the variation selector codepoint with the preceding codepoint you want to render. In my case, that’s U+FE0E, Variation Selector-15. Using my source document’s backlink HTML markup as an example:

<a href="#fnref:1" class="reversefootnote" role="doc-backlink">
  ↩︎
</a>

And, if I wanted, I could do the opposite: force the emoji presentation, using U+FE0F, Variation Selector-16.

So, I can fix this issue for myself. I’m fortunate that I build the software that generates this website; and I’m fortunate that the library I use to generate HTML from my Markdown source documents (Kramdown) has a configurable footnote backlink glyph. There are authors using the Mistune library (for example, via the Bear blogging platform),4 or perhaps some other niche content rendering library, that don’t have the same luxury as of this writing.

Some less reliable solutions

If you’re in a position where you don’t control the software that builds HTML from your source documents, and, for some reason, it’s not possible to insert a variation selector character where you want it, there are some other things you can try. (At least for applications that use CSS for document presentation, like web browsers.)

font-variant-emoji

The font-variant-emoji CSS property is not currently supported in Safari. Hopefully it will be soon. It allows you to specify the preferred presentation of unicode characters for which ever DOM elements you like:

<article style="font-variant-emoji: text;">
  <span>Umbrella ⛱</span>, or
  <span style="font-variant-emoji: emoji;">umbrella ⛱</span>.
</article>

But I don’t think this can ever be a foolproof solution. Even wide web browser support doesn’t fully account for web content being presented by non–web browser applications like feed readers, and so on.

font-family: monospace

If you want an even less reliable solution, setting the font-family CSS property to the value monospace sometimes works:

<article>
  <span style="font-family: monospace;">Umbrella ⛱</span>, or
  <span>umbrella ⛱</span>.
</article>

But this only forces emoji-presented characters to be text-presented characters. And monospace is a generic font specifier that may look different from device to device.

  1. Here’s a short list of the most interesting links I found while looking into this:

    • A Hacker News thread somewhere on the post about Google’s Noto Emoji font.
    • “How to Prevent Unicode Characters from Rendering as Emojis in HTML with JavaScript: Force Classic Glyphs” on XJavascript.com.
    • A question about “previous” and “next” arrows as rendered on iOS devices on Meta Stack Exchange.
    • An issue on the Kramdown Markdown library from April 2015, still getting pings as late as May 2026.

    ↩︎

  2. Holbrook, Ned: “Emoji Subcommittee Report for UTC #171 (2022Q2),” 22 April 2022 [PDF]:

    A suggestion was made to emojify an existing character rather than add a new one. Regarding the proposed U+1FAAF KHANDA, Chris Hennick suggested the existing U+262C ADI SHAKTI be emojified instead. The ESC notes that doing so could affect the rendering of documents using ADI SHAKTI and that the UTC prefers distinct characters for new emoji for this reason [emphasis added]. ↩︎

  3. Bettencourt, Rebecca; Ewell, Doug: “Proposal to disunify Symbols for Legacy Computing from emoji,” 14 October 2023 [PDF]:

    The UTC has determined that unifying text and emoji characters was a mistake, and that attempts will be made to prevent further such unifications. The Emoji Subcommittee even has a standing agreement with the UTC to only propose new characters rather than adding the emoji property to existing characters, and to avoid creating new presentation sequences. The Symbols for Legacy Computing proposals have resulted in the opposite case of needing to use existing emoji characters as non-emoji; regardless, the requirement to prevent emoji unification still applies. Upon reviewing Buff’s proposal, the Script Ad-Hoc (including the members most opposed to the Symbols for Legacy Computing proposals) came to the consensus that the best approach is to propose these as separate characters. ↩︎

  4. As evidence, here’s a link to an archived version of “Cities need more trees” on the Bear blogging platform by the creator of Bear, Herman Martinus, which uses Mistune to render HTML from Markdown. Currently, when I visit the webpage on iOS I see the full-colour emoji version of Leftwards Arrow with Hook instead of the pictogram. But in Firefox on my Linux distribution I see the pictogram. ↩︎
content_text:
From my phone, I noticed that my website was displaying footnote backlink glyphs as emoji in my RSS reader. It had not always been this way. I opened the same page from a desktop browser and saw what I expected to see: a text-like pictograph, in my article body’s font face. No emoji to be found. I checked the HTML document source: ↩ The RSS feed XML contained the same. So, that can be presented as either text or an emoji? &x8617; is an HTML representation of the Unicode codepoint U+21A9, Leftwards Arrow with Hook. This led me to many relevant threads, pastes, and blog posts1 in which others had observed similar issues with Leftwards Arrow with Hook and other, similar navigation-decoration characters. We were all reacting to U+21A9 as rendered without a Unicode standardized variation sequence specified. So it’s up to whatever program is rendering U+21A9 to decide what to do. Which version, the text or the emoji, should U+21A9 render. I found the correct solution and some less correct solutions to this issue. Before I describe them, I want to explore a slightly larger problem introduced by variation selectors. The downstream effects For meta-ish content characters like backlink glyphs, the way forward is clear for people who are technical: fix and/or configure the thing that builds your web pages. Or complain in the direction of the people who can fix and/or configure the thing. For many of the other characters, it’s more complicated. Many of these characters are used in body content to be expressive. Rendering the text presentation instead of the emoji presentation, at worst, could be a mistranslation. The technical standard (see the emoji-data links, and specifically emoji-data.txt) lists over 1,000 Unicode codepoints that have both text and emoji presentations. Codepoint Glyph As text(+U+FE0E) As emoji(+U+FE0F) U+00A9 © ©︎ ©️ U+00AE ® ®︎ ®️ U+2328 ⌨ ⌨︎ ⌨️ U+25B6 ▶ ▶︎ ▶️ U+2603 ☃ ☃︎ ☃️ U+2620 ☠ ☠︎ ☠️ U+267B ♻ ♻︎ ♻️ U+1F310 🌐 🌐︎ 🌐️ U+1F312 🌒 🌒︎ 🌒️ U+1F37C 🍼 🍼︎ 🍼️ U+1F441 👁 👁︎ 👁️ U+1F509 🔉 🔉︎ 🔉️ U+1F590 🖐 🖐︎ 🖐️ U+1F635 😵 😵︎ 😵️ U+1F636 😶 😶︎ 😶️ Table 1 A small sampling of the 1,000+ Unicode codepoints that have both text and emoji presentations. The Glyph column may look different to you, depending on the application you're using to read this article. I would hope the the +U+FE0E and +U+FE0F columns do render the same way for everyone. There is no obvious, cut-and-dry user experience for writers here. Pictographs and emoji are regular text as far as most programs are concerned, and variation selectors remain invisible unless you go looking. Did the author mean to present an umbrella ⛱︎ or an umbrella ⛱️ ? We don’t know what their writing tool presented to them during editing. They don’t know that we don’t see what they think we see when we’re reading the thing. In a perfect world, all software knows about and inserts variation selectors for the user. In reality, we know that software is bad. I found documentation that indicates that the Unicode Technical Committee is aware that variation selectors are complicated,2 and maybe weren’t the best strategy.3 But what’s done is done and can’t be undone when it comes to the Unicode Standard. The most correct solution You can specify, in your source document, which version you want rendered. You do this by combining the variation selector codepoint with the preceding codepoint you want to render. In my case, that’s U+FE0E, Variation Selector-15. Using my source document’s backlink HTML markup as an example: ↩︎ And, if I wanted, I could do the opposite: force the emoji presentation, using U+FE0F, Variation Selector-16. ↩︎ Fig. 1 The pictogram via U+21A9 U+FE0E. ↩ ↩️ Fig. 2 The emoji via U+21A9 alone, next to the emoji rendered via U+21A9 U+FE0F, where U-FE0F, Variation Selector-16 coerces the glyph to use its full-colour emoji variant. Fig. 3 Here's the image of the pictogram version and the iOS emoji version as rendered via U+21A9 alone. As I've already mentioned, which variant you see sans-selector can differ per application. So, I can fix this issue for myself. I’m fortunate that I build the software that generates this website; and I’m fortunate that the library I use to generate HTML from my Markdown source documents (Kramdown) has a configurable footnote backlink glyph. There are authors using the Mistune library (for example, via the Bear blogging platform),4 or perhaps some other niche content rendering library, that don’t have the same luxury as of this writing. Some less reliable solutions If you’re in a position where you don’t control the software that builds HTML from your source documents, and, for some reason, it’s not possible to insert a variation selector character where you want it, there are some other things you can try. (At least for applications that use CSS for document presentation, like web browsers.) font-variant-emoji The font-variant-emoji CSS property is not currently supported in Safari. Hopefully it will be soon. It allows you to specify the preferred presentation of unicode characters for which ever DOM elements you like:
Umbrella ⛱, or umbrella ⛱.
But I don’t think this can ever be a foolproof solution. Even wide web browser support doesn’t fully account for web content being presented by non–web browser applications like feed readers, and so on. font-family: monospace If you want an even less reliable solution, setting the font-family CSS property to the value monospace sometimes works:
Umbrella ⛱, or umbrella ⛱.
But this only forces emoji-presented characters to be text-presented characters. And monospace is a generic font specifier that may look different from device to device. Codepoint Solution Glyph +U+FE0E +U+FE0F U+26F1 none ⛱ ⛱︎ ⛱️ U+21A9 none ↩ ↩︎ ↩️ U+26F1 font-variant-emoji: text ⛱ ⛱︎ ⛱️ U+21A9 font-variant-emoji: text ↩ ↩︎ ↩️ U+26F1 font-variant-emoji: emoji ⛱ ⛱︎ ⛱️ U+21A9 font-variant-emoji: emoji ↩ ↩︎ ↩️ U+26F1 font-family: monospace ⛱ ⛱︎ ⛱️ U+21A9 font-family: monospace ↩ ↩︎ ↩️ Table 2 A table demonstrating the less reliable solutions. Here’s a short list of the most interesting links I found while looking into this: A Hacker News thread somewhere on the post about Google’s Noto Emoji font. “How to Prevent Unicode Characters from Rendering as Emojis in HTML with JavaScript: Force Classic Glyphs” on XJavascript.com. A question about “previous” and “next” arrows as rendered on iOS devices on Meta Stack Exchange. An issue on the Kramdown Markdown library from April 2015, still getting pings as late as May 2026. ↩︎ Holbrook, Ned: “Emoji Subcommittee Report for UTC #171 (2022Q2),” 22 April 2022 [PDF]: A suggestion was made to emojify an existing character rather than add a new one. Regarding the proposed U+1FAAF KHANDA, Chris Hennick suggested the existing U+262C ADI SHAKTI be emojified instead. The ESC notes that doing so could affect the rendering of documents using ADI SHAKTI and that the UTC prefers distinct characters for new emoji for this reason [emphasis added]. ↩︎ Bettencourt, Rebecca; Ewell, Doug: “Proposal to disunify Symbols for Legacy Computing from emoji,” 14 October 2023 [PDF]: The UTC has determined that unifying text and emoji characters was a mistake, and that attempts will be made to prevent further such unifications. The Emoji Subcommittee even has a standing agreement with the UTC to only propose new characters rather than adding the emoji property to existing characters, and to avoid creating new presentation sequences. The Symbols for Legacy Computing proposals have resulted in the opposite case of needing to use existing emoji characters as non-emoji; regardless, the requirement to prevent emoji unification still applies. Upon reviewing Buff’s proposal, the Script Ad-Hoc (including the members most opposed to the Symbols for Legacy Computing proposals) came to the consensus that the best approach is to propose these as separate characters. ↩︎ As evidence, here’s a link to an archived version of “Cities need more trees” on the Bear blogging platform by the creator of Bear, Herman Martinus, which uses Mistune to render HTML from Markdown. Currently, when I visit the webpage on iOS I see the full-colour emoji version of Leftwards Arrow with Hook instead of the pictogram. But in Firefox on my Linux distribution I see the pictogram. ↩︎

sentences.zh: 使用我的源文档的反向链接 HTML 标记作为示例：

blocks.html_en: And, if I wanted, I could do the opposite: force the emoji presentation, using U+FE0F, Variation Selector-16.

sentences.en: And, if I wanted, I could do the opposite: force the emoji presentation, using U+FE0F, Variation Selector-16.
sentences.zh: 而且，如果我愿意，我可以做相反的事情：使用 U+FE0F、Variation Selector-16 强制显示表情符号。

sentences.en: Fig. 1 The pictogram via U+21A9 U+FE0E.
sentences.zh: 图 1 通过 U+21A9 U+FE0E 的象形图。

sentences.en: Fig. 1 The pictogram via U+21A9 U+FE0E.
sentences.zh: 图 1 通过 U+21A9 U+FE0E 的象形图。

sentences.en: Fig. 2 The emoji via U+21A9 alone, next to the emoji rendered via U+21A9 U+FE0F, where U-FE0F, Variation Selector-16 coerces the glyph to use its full-colour emoji variant.
sentences.zh: 图 2 单独通过 U+21A9 呈现的表情符号，旁边是通过 U+21A9 U+FE0F 渲染的表情符号，其中 U-FE0F、变体选择器 16 强制字形使用其全彩表情符号变体。

blocks.html_en: Fig. 2 The emoji via U+21A9 alone, next to the emoji rendered via U+21A9 U+FE0F, where U-FE0F, Variation Selector-16 coerces the glyph to use its full-colour emoji variant.

sentences.en: Fig. 2 The emoji via U+21A9 alone, next to the emoji rendered via U+21A9 U+FE0F, where U-FE0F, Variation Selector-16 coerces the glyph to use its full-colour emoji variant.
sentences.zh: 图 2 单独通过 U+21A9 呈现的表情符号，旁边是通过 U+21A9 U+FE0F 渲染的表情符号，其中 U-FE0F、变体选择器 16 强制字形使用其全彩表情符号变体。

sentences.en: Fig. 3 Here's the image of the pictogram version and the iOS emoji version as rendered via U+21A9 alone.
sentences.zh: 图 3 这是单独通过 U+21A9 渲染的象形图版本和 iOS 表情符号版本的图像。

sentences.en: As I've already mentioned, which variant you see sans-selector can differ per application.
sentences.zh: 正如我已经提到的，您看到的 sans-selector 变体可能因应用程序而异。

blocks.html_en: Fig. 3 Here's the image of the pictogram version and the iOS emoji version as rendered via U+21A9 alone. As I've already mentioned, which variant you see sans-selector can differ per application.

sentences.en: Fig. 3 Here's the image of the pictogram version and the iOS emoji version as rendered via U+21A9 alone.
sentences.zh: 图 3 这是单独通过 U+21A9 渲染的象形图版本和 iOS 表情符号版本的图像。

sentences.en: As I've already mentioned, which variant you see sans-selector can differ per application.
sentences.zh: 正如我已经提到的，您看到的 sans-selector 变体可能因应用程序而异。

blocks.html_en: So, I can fix this issue for myself. I’m fortunate that I build the software that generates this website; and I’m fortunate that the library I use to generate HTML from my Markdown source documents (Kramdown) has a configurable footnote backlink glyph. There are authors using the Mistune library (for example, via the Bear blogging platform),4 or perhaps some other niche content rendering library, that don’t have the same luxury as of this writing.

sentences.en: So, I can fix this issue for myself.
sentences.zh: 所以，我可以自己解决这个问题。

sentences.en: I’m fortunate that I build the software that generates this website; and I’m fortunate that the library I use to generate HTML from my Markdown source documents (Kramdown) has a configurable footnote backlink glyph.
sentences.zh: 我很幸运能够构建生成这个网站的软件；我很幸运，我用来从 Markdown 源文档 (Kramdown) 生成 HTML 的库有一个可配置的脚注反向链接字形。

sentences.en: There are authors using the Mistune library (for example, via the Bear blogging platform),4 or perhaps some other niche content rendering library, that don’t have the same luxury as of this writing.
sentences.zh: 有些作者使用 Mistune 库（例如，通过 Bear 博客平台）4 或其他一些利基内容渲染库，但它们没有撰写本文时那样的奢侈。

sentences.en: Some less reliable solutions
sentences.zh: 一些不太可靠的解决方案

blocks.html_en: If you’re in a position where you don’t control the software that builds HTML from your source documents, and, for some reason, it’s not possible to insert a variation selector character where you want it, there are some other things you can try. (At least for applications that use CSS for document presentation, like web browsers.)

sentences.en: If you’re in a position where you don’t control the software that builds HTML from your source documents, and, for some reason, it’s not possible to insert a variation selector character where you want it, there are some other things you can try.
sentences.zh: 如果您无法控制从源文档构建 HTML 的软件，并且由于某种原因无法在您想要的位置插入变体选择器字符，那么您可以尝试其他一些方法。

sentences.en: (At least for applications that use CSS for document presentation, like web browsers.)
sentences.zh: （至少对于使用 CSS 进行文档呈现的应用程序来说是这样，例如 Web 浏览器。）

sentences.en: font-variant-emoji
sentences.zh: 字体变体表情符号

blocks.html_en: The font-variant-emoji CSS property is not currently supported in Safari. Hopefully it will be soon. It allows you to specify the preferred presentation of unicode characters for which ever DOM elements you like:

sentences.en: The font-variant-emoji CSS property is not currently supported in Safari.
sentences.zh: Safari 目前不支持 font-variant-emoji CSS 属性。

sentences.en: Hopefully it will be soon.
sentences.zh: 希望很快就会实现。

sentences.en: It allows you to specify the preferred presentation of unicode characters for which ever DOM elements you like:
sentences.zh: 它允许您为您喜欢的 DOM 元素指定 unicode 字符的首选表示形式：

blocks.html_en: But I don’t think this can ever be a foolproof solution. Even wide web browser support doesn’t fully account for web content being presented by non–web browser applications like feed readers, and so on.

sentences.en: But I don’t think this can ever be a foolproof solution.
sentences.zh: 但我认为这永远不会是一个万无一失的解决方案。

sentences.en: Even wide web browser support doesn’t fully account for web content being presented by non–web browser applications like feed readers, and so on.
sentences.zh: 即使是广泛的网络浏览器支持也不能完全考虑非网络浏览器应用程序（例如提要阅读器等）呈现的网络内容。

sentences.en: font-family: monospace
sentences.zh: 字体系列：等宽字体

blocks.html_en: If you want an even less reliable solution, setting the font-family CSS property to the value monospace sometimes works:

sentences.en: If you want an even less reliable solution, setting the font-family CSS property to the value monospace sometimes works:
sentences.zh: 如果您想要一个更不可靠的解决方案，将 font-family CSS 属性设置为值 monospace 有时可行：

blocks.html_en: But this only forces emoji-presented characters to be text-presented characters. And monospace is a generic font specifier that may look different from device to device.

sentences.en: But this only forces emoji-presented characters to be text-presented characters.
sentences.zh: 但这只会强制表情符号呈现的字符成为文本呈现的字符。

sentences.en: And monospace is a generic font specifier that may look different from device to device.
sentences.zh: 等宽字体是一种通用字体说明符，不同设备的外观可能有所不同。

sentences.en: Codepoint
sentences.zh: 代码点

sentences.en: Solution
sentences.zh: 解决方案

sentences.zh: 字体变体表情符号：文本

sentences.en: font-variant-emoji: text
sentences.zh: 字体变体表情符号：文本

sentences.en: font-variant-emoji: emoji
sentences.zh: 字体变体表情符号：表情符号

sentences.en: font-variant-emoji: emoji
sentences.zh: 字体变体表情符号：表情符号

sentences.zh: 字体系列：等宽字体

sentences.en: font-family: monospace
sentences.zh: 字体系列：等宽字体

sentences.zh: 表 2 展示不太可靠的解决方案的表格。

sentences.en: Table 2 A table demonstrating the less reliable solutions.
sentences.zh: 表 2 展示不太可靠的解决方案的表格。

blocks.html_en:
• Here’s a short list of the most interesting links I found while looking into this:

    • A Hacker News thread somewhere on the post about Google’s Noto Emoji font.
    • “How to Prevent Unicode Characters from Rendering as Emojis in HTML with JavaScript: Force Classic Glyphs” on XJavascript.com.
    • A question about “previous” and “next” arrows as rendered on iOS devices on Meta Stack Exchange.
    • An issue on the Kramdown Markdown library from April 2015, still getting pings as late as May 2026.

    ↩︎

sentences.en: Here’s a short list of the most interesting links I found while looking into this: A Hacker News thread somewhere on the post about Google’s Noto Emoji font.
sentences.zh: 以下是我在研究此内容时发现的最有趣链接的简短列表：关于 Google 的 Noto Emoji 字体的帖子中某处的黑客新闻主题。

sentences.en: “How to Prevent Unicode Characters from Rendering as Emojis in HTML with JavaScript: Force Classic Glyphs” on XJavascript.com.
sentences.zh: XJavascript.com 上的“如何使用 JavaScript 防止 Unicode 字符在 HTML 中呈现为表情符号：强制经典字形”。

sentences.en: A question about “previous” and “next” arrows as rendered on iOS devices on Meta Stack Exchange.
sentences.zh: 关于 Meta Stack Exchange 上 iOS 设备上呈现的“上一个”和“下一个”箭头的问题。

sentences.en: An issue on the Kramdown Markdown library from April 2015, still getting pings as late as May 2026. ↩︎
sentences.zh: 从 2015 年 4 月起，Kramdown Markdown 库上出现了一个问题，直到 2026 年 5 月仍然收到 ping。↩︎

blocks.html_en:
• Holbrook, Ned: “Emoji Subcommittee Report for UTC #171 (2022Q2),” 22 April 2022 [PDF]:

    A suggestion was made to emojify an existing character rather than add a new one. Regarding the proposed U+1FAAF KHANDA, Chris Hennick suggested the existing U+262C ADI SHAKTI be emojified instead. The ESC notes that doing so could affect the rendering of documents using ADI SHAKTI and that the UTC prefers distinct characters for new emoji for this reason [emphasis added]. ↩︎

sentences.en: Holbrook, Ned: “Emoji Subcommittee Report for UTC #171 (2022Q2),” 22 April 2022 [PDF]: A suggestion was made to emojify an existing character rather than add a new one.
sentences.zh: Holbrook, Ned：“UTC #171 表情符号小组委员会报告 (2022Q2)”，2022 年 4 月 22 日 [PDF]：有人建议对现有角色进行表情符号化，而不是添加新角色。

sentences.en: Regarding the proposed U+1FAAF KHANDA, Chris Hennick suggested the existing U+262C ADI SHAKTI be emojified instead.
sentences.zh: 关于拟议的 U+1FAAF KHANDA，Chris Hennick 建议将现有的 U+262C ADI SHAKTI 改为表情符号。

sentences.en: The ESC notes that doing so could affect the rendering of documents using ADI SHAKTI and that the UTC prefers distinct characters for new emoji for this reason [emphasis added]. ↩︎
sentences.zh: ESC 指出，这样做可能会影响使用 ADI SHAKTI 呈现文档，并且出于这个原因，UTC 更喜欢新表情符号使用不同的字符[强调]。 ↩︎

blocks.html_en: A suggestion was made to emojify an existing character rather than add a new one. Regarding the proposed U+1FAAF KHANDA, Chris Hennick suggested the existing U+262C ADI SHAKTI be emojified instead. The ESC notes that doing so could affect the rendering of documents using ADI SHAKTI and that the UTC prefers distinct characters for new emoji for this reason [emphasis added]. ↩︎

sentences.en: A suggestion was made to emojify an existing character rather than add a new one.
sentences.zh: 有人建议对现有角色进行表情符号化，而不是添加新角色。

sentences.en: Regarding the proposed U+1FAAF KHANDA, Chris Hennick suggested the existing U+262C ADI SHAKTI be emojified instead.
sentences.zh: 关于拟议的 U+1FAAF KHANDA，Chris Hennick 建议将现有的 U+262C ADI SHAKTI 改为表情符号。

sentences.en: The ESC notes that doing so could affect the rendering of documents using ADI SHAKTI and that the UTC prefers distinct characters for new emoji for this reason [emphasis added]. ↩︎
sentences.zh: ESC 指出，这样做可能会影响使用 ADI SHAKTI 呈现文档，并且出于这个原因，UTC 更喜欢新表情符号使用不同的字符[强调]。 ↩︎

blocks.html_en:
• Bettencourt, Rebecca; Ewell, Doug: “Proposal to disunify Symbols for Legacy Computing from emoji,” 14 October 2023 [PDF]:

    The UTC has determined that unifying text and emoji characters was a mistake, and that attempts will be made to prevent further such unifications. The Emoji Subcommittee even has a standing agreement with the UTC to only propose new characters rather than adding the emoji property to existing characters, and to avoid creating new presentation sequences. The Symbols for Legacy Computing proposals have resulted in the opposite case of needing to use existing emoji characters as non-emoji; regardless, the requirement to prevent emoji unification still applies. Upon reviewing Buff’s proposal, the Script Ad-Hoc (including the members most opposed to the Symbols for Legacy Computing proposals) came to the consensus that the best approach is to propose these as separate characters. ↩︎

sentences.en: Bettencourt, Rebecca; Ewell, Doug: “Proposal to disunify Symbols for Legacy Computing from emoji,” 14 October 2023 [PDF]: The UTC has determined that unifying text and emoji characters was a mistake, and that attempts will be made to prevent further such unifications.
sentences.zh: 丽贝卡·贝当古； Ewell, Doug：“关于将传统计算符号与表情符号分开的提案”，2023 年 10 月 14 日 [PDF]：UTC 已确定统一文本和表情符号字符是一个错误，并将尝试阻止进一步的此类统一。

sentences.en: The Emoji Subcommittee even has a standing agreement with the UTC to only propose new characters rather than adding the emoji property to existing characters, and to avoid creating new presentation sequences.
sentences.zh: 表情符号小组委员会甚至与 UTC 达成长期协议，仅提议新字符，而不是向现有字符添加表情符号属性，并避免创建新的呈现序列。

sentences.en: The Symbols for Legacy Computing proposals have resulted in the opposite case of needing to use existing emoji characters as non-emoji; regardless, the requirement to prevent emoji unification still applies.
sentences.zh: 传统计算符号提案导致了相反的情况，即需要将现有表情符号字符用作非表情符号；无论如何，防止表情符号统一的要求仍然适用。

sentences.en: Upon reviewing Buff’s proposal, the Script Ad-Hoc (including the members most opposed to the Symbols for Legacy Computing proposals) came to the consensus that the best approach is to propose these as separate characters. ↩︎
sentences.zh: 在审查了 Buff 的提案后，Script Ad-Hoc（包括最反对遗留计算符号提案的成员）达成共识，即最好的方法是将它们作为单独的角色提出。 ↩︎

Upon reviewing Buff’s proposal, the Script Ad-Hoc (including the members most opposed to the Symbols for Legacy Computing proposals) came to the consensus that the best approach is to propose these as separate characters. ↩︎

sentences.zh: 传统计算符号提案导致了相反的情况，即需要将现有表情符号字符用作非表情符号；无论如何，防止表情符号统一的要求仍然适用。

blocks.html_en: • As evidence, here’s a link to an archived version of “Cities need more trees” on the Bear blogging platform by the creator of Bear, Herman Martinus, which uses Mistune to render HTML from Markdown. Currently, when I visit the webpage on iOS I see the full-colour emoji version of Leftwards Arrow with Hook instead of the pictogram. But in Firefox on my Linux distribution I see the pictogram. ↩︎

sentences.en: As evidence, here’s a link to an archived version of “Cities need more trees” on the Bear blogging platform by the creator of Bear, Herman Martinus, which uses Mistune to render HTML from Markdown.

sentences.en: Currently, when I visit the webpage on iOS I see the full-colour emoji version of Leftwards Arrow with Hook instead of the pictogram.

sentences.en: But in Firefox on my Linux distribution I see the pictogram. ↩︎
sentences.zh: 但在我的 Linux 发行版上的 Firefox 中，我看到了象形图。 ↩︎

content_html_zh:
通过手机，我注意到我的网站在 RSS 阅读器中将脚注反向链接字形显示为表情符号。 情况并非一直如此。 我从桌面浏览器打开同一页面，看到了我期望看到的内容：文章正文字体中的类似文本的象形文字。 找不到表情符号。 我检查了HTML文档来源：

<a href="#fnref:1" class="reversefootnote" role="doc-backlink">
  ↩
</a>

RSS feed XML 包含相同的内容。 那么，它可以呈现为文本或表情符号吗？ &x8617;是 Unicode 代码点 U+21A9（带钩向左箭头）的 HTML 表示形式。 这让我找到了许多相关的主题、粘贴和博客文章1，其中其他人观察到带有钩子的向左箭头和其他类似的导航装饰字符的类似问题。 我们都对 U+21A9 做出了反应，因为它没有指定 Unicode 标准化变体序列。 因此，由渲染 U+21A9 的程序来决定要做什么。 U+21A9 应该渲染哪个版本，文本还是表情符号。

我找到了这个问题的正确解决方案和一些不太正确的解决方案。 在描述它们之前，我想探讨一下由变体选择器引入的一个稍大的问题。

下游影响

对于像反向链接字形这样的元内容字符，对于技术人员来说前进的道路是明确的：修复和/或配置构建网页的东西。 或者向可以修复和/或配置该问题的人员投诉。

对于许多其他角色来说，情况要复杂得多。 其中许多字符都用在正文内容中以表达情感。 在最坏的情况下，呈现文本呈现而不是表情符号呈现可能是误译。 技术标准（请参阅表情符号数据链接，特别是 emoji-data.txt）列出了 1,000 多个具有文本和表情符号表示形式的 Unicode 代码点。

对于作家来说，这里没有明显的、一成不变的用户体验。 就大多数程序而言，象形文字和表情符号是常规文本，除非您去查看，否则变体选择器保持不可见。 作者是想送一把雨伞⛱︎还是一把雨伞⛱️？ 我们不知道他们的写作工具在编辑过程中向他们呈现了什么。 他们不知道当我们阅读这些内容时，我们并没有看到他们认为我们看到的东西。 在完美的世界中，所有软件都知道并为用户插入变体选择器。 事实上，我们知道软件很糟糕。

我发现的文档表明 Unicode 技术委员会意识到变体选择器很复杂，2 并且可能不是最好的策略。3 但就 Unicode 标准而言，已经完成的事情无法撤销。

最正确的解决方案

您可以在源文档中指定要呈现的版本。 您可以通过将变体选择器代码点与要渲染的前面的代码点组合来实现此目的。 就我而言，这是 U+FE0E，Variation Selector-15。 使用我的源文档的反向链接 HTML 标记作为示例：

<a href="#fnref:1" class="reversefootnote" role="doc-backlink">
  ↩︎
</a>

而且，如果我愿意，我可以做相反的事情：使用 U+FE0F、Variation Selector-16 强制显示表情符号。

所以，我可以自己解决这个问题。 我很幸运能够构建生成这个网站的软件；我很幸运，我用来从 Markdown 源文档 (Kramdown) 生成 HTML 的库有一个可配置的脚注反向链接字形。 有些作者使用 Mistune 库（例如，通过 Bear 博客平台）4 或其他一些利基内容渲染库，但它们没有撰写本文时那样的奢侈。

一些不太可靠的解决方案

如果您无法控制从源文档构建 HTML 的软件，并且由于某种原因无法在您想要的位置插入变体选择器字符，那么您可以尝试其他一些方法。 （至少对于使用 CSS 进行文档呈现的应用程序来说是这样，例如 Web 浏览器。）

字体变体表情符号

Safari 目前不支持 font-variant-emoji CSS 属性。 希望很快就会实现。 它允许您为您喜欢的 DOM 元素指定 unicode 字符的首选表示形式：

<article style="font-variant-emoji: text;">
  <span>Umbrella ⛱</span>, or
  <span style="font-variant-emoji: emoji;">umbrella ⛱</span>.
</article>

但我认为这永远不会是一个万无一失的解决方案。 即使是广泛的网络浏览器支持也不能完全考虑非网络浏览器应用程序（例如提要阅读器等）呈现的网络内容。

字体系列：等宽字体

如果您想要一个更不可靠的解决方案，将 font-family CSS 属性设置为值 monospace 有时可行：

<article>
  <span style="font-family: monospace;">Umbrella ⛱</span>, or
  <span>umbrella ⛱</span>.
</article>

但这只会强制表情符号呈现的字符成为文本呈现的字符。 等宽字体是一种通用字体说明符，不同设备的外观可能有所不同。

  1. 以下是我在研究此内容时发现的最有趣链接的简短列表：关于 Google 的 Noto Emoji 字体的帖子中某处的黑客新闻主题。 XJavascript.com 上的“如何使用 JavaScript 防止 Unicode 字符在 HTML 中呈现为表情符号：强制经典字形”。 关于 Meta Stack Exchange 上 iOS 设备上呈现的“上一个”和“下一个”箭头的问题。 从 2015 年 4 月起，Kramdown Markdown 库上出现了一个问题，直到 2026 年 5 月仍然收到 ping。↩︎
  2. Holbrook, Ned：“UTC #171 表情符号小组委员会报告 (2022Q2)”，2022 年 4 月 22 日 [PDF]：有人建议对现有角色进行表情符号化，而不是添加新角色。 关于拟议的 U+1FAAF KHANDA，Chris Hennick 建议将现有的 U+262C ADI SHAKTI 改为表情符号。 ESC 指出，这样做可能会影响使用 ADI SHAKTI 呈现文档，并且出于这个原因，UTC 更喜欢新表情符号使用不同的字符[强调]。 ↩︎
  3. 丽贝卡·贝当古； Ewell, Doug：“关于将传统计算符号与表情符号分开的提案”，2023 年 10 月 14 日 [PDF]：UTC 已确定统一文本和表情符号字符是一个错误，并将尝试阻止进一步的此类统一。 表情符号小组委员会甚至与 UTC 达成长期协议，仅提议新字符，而不是向现有字符添加表情符号属性，并避免创建新的呈现序列。 传统计算符号提案导致了相反的情况，即需要将现有表情符号字符用作非表情符号；无论如何，防止表情符号统一的要求仍然适用。 在审查了 Buff 的提案后，Script Ad-Hoc（包括最反对遗留计算符号提案的成员）达成共识，即最好的方法是将它们作为单独的角色提出。 ↩︎
  4. 作为证据，这里有一个由 Bear 的创建者 Herman Martinus 在 Bear 博客平台上发布的“城市需要更多树木”的存档版本的链接，该版本使用 Mistune 从 Markdown 渲染 HTML。 目前，当我在 iOS 上访问网页时，我看到的是带有钩子的向左箭头的全彩表情符号版本，而不是象形图。 但在我的 Linux 发行版上的 Firefox 中，我看到了象形图。 ↩︎
